title: How to Get a Police Certificate – The Comoros Islands - Canada.ca
content:
## How to Get a Police Certificate – The Comoros Islands
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.
Do I need to provide a Police Certificate with my Application for Permanent Residence? Yes
Name of document(s) to get: Bulletin N o 3 Extrait de casier judiciaire

## How to get a Police Certificate:

### If you are a citizen of The Comoros Islands:

#### If you live in The Comoros Islands:
You must apply in person to the “ Tribunal de première instance ” of Moroni if you are from Grande Comoros (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Your original birth certificate
- Your original passport
- Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names
- Place of residence in Comoros

#### If you live outside of The Comoros Islands:
You must apply to the “ Tribunal de première instance ” of Moroni if you are from Grande Comoros (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Copy of your birth certificate
- Copy of your passport
- Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names
- Place of residence while in Comoros

### If you are not a citizen of The Comoros Islands:

#### If you live in The Comoros Islands:
You must apply in person to the “ Tribunal de première instance ” of Moroni if you are from Grande Comoros (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Your original birth certificate
- Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names
- Certified copy of passport and copies of visa/permits showing legal residence in Comoros
- Place of residence in Comoros

#### If you live outside of The Comoros Islands:
You must apply to the “ Tribunal de première instance ” of Moroni if you are from Grande Comoros (see Special notes and considerations for complete address). You must bring:
- Copy of your birth certificate
- Your father’s full name and your mother’s maiden and married names
- Certified copy of passport and copies of visa/permits showing legal residence in Comoros
- Place of residence while in Comoros

### Information and documents you need:
All information and documents needed are explained above

### Special notes and considerations
Greffier en Chef Tribunal de Moroni Comoros
- For married women a marriage certificate must be provided
- Non-residents may also apply in person to the Master of Justice, at the Comoros Embassy in their country of residence
